bad_debt_loss_allocation.description: How bad-debt liquidation affects suppliers (see [`crate::model::BadDebtLossAllocation`]). Default: defer to `deficit_underlying`.

borrower_required_attrs.description: Attribute names for borrowing: sender must have all of these. - Empty list = no attribute required.

commit_market_id.description: When set, withdraw with commit_funds: true will emit MsgCommitFundsRequest to this Provenance exchange market. None = no on-chain commit.

lender_required_attrs.description: Attribute names for lending: sender must have all of these (Provenance attributes). - Empty list = no attribute required.

liquidation_bonus_rate.description: Liquidation bonus rate: max collateral value = repay value × this (e.g. 1.02 = 2% bonus). - Must be > 1.

min_borrow.description: Minimum amount per borrow (in lending denom base units). - Must be at least 1. - Independent of [`InstantiateMsg::min_lend`].

min_lend.description: Minimum amount per lend (in lending denom base units). - Must be at least 1. - Used to avoid dust and zero-size lends.

price_oracle_address.description: Price oracle contract address. - Must be a valid bech32 address.

BadDebtLossAllocation.description: How bad-debt liquidation (residual scaled debt after collateral exhausted) hits suppliers.

oneOf.description: Book `deficit_underlying`; reduce via **EliminateDeficit** or **SocializeDeficit** (pro-rata `liquidity_index` haircut + lower deficit).
In the same liquidation tx, apply a pro-rata `liquidity_index` haircut for the bad-debt amount (no `deficit_underlying` increment for that slice).

h.description: Discount for valuation: collateral value = price × amount × haircut. None = 100% (no discount; full value).

Decimal256.description:
A fixed-point decimal value with 18 fractional digits, i.e. Decimal256(1_000_000_000_000_000_000) == 1.0

The greatest possible value that can be represented is 115792089237316195423570985008687907853269984665640564039457.584007913129639935 (which is (2^256 - 1) / 10^18)

RepoTokenConfig.description: How the pool binds to the repo (receipt) CW20: use an existing contract or instantiate a new one in the same transaction.

oneOf.description: Use a repo token contract that is already deployed (e.g. shared or pre-created). Address is validated and stored on instantiate; no `SubMsg` / `reply`.

Instantiate a new `repo_token_cw20` from this code id; the pool emits `WasmMsg::Instantiate` as a `SubMsg` and binds the address in `reply`. The SubMsg uses [`democratized_prime_lib::repo_token::InstantiateMsg`]; name, symbol, and decimals are checked with [`democratized_prime_lib::repo_token::validate_repo_token_meta`] (same as `repo_token_cw20`’s `instantiate`).

repo_token_decimals.description: CW20 decimals (same validation; typically same as lending denom).
